message:
fix(export): drop dangling deps from issue_metrics counts (audit follow-up)

Self-audit on 0bd7bdb (#143/#144 fix) found a consistency hole: the
materialized view's blocks_ids / blocked_by_ids subqueries
INNER JOIN against the `issues` table, so any dependency edge whose
endpoint isn't a known issue is dropped from the id list. The Go-side
`isResolved("missing-id")` returned false (zero-value Status doesn't
match closed/tombstone), so the same edge would still get *counted*
in issue_metrics.blocks_count / blocked_by_count.

Net result: a row with one real blocker plus one dangling dep would
show `blocked_by_count = 2` while `blocked_by_ids = "real-id"`
(length 1) — the count column and the id list disagreeing on the
same surface.

Tightened the helper from "is the endpoint resolved?" to
"is the endpoint a *known active issue?" and pruned the count loop
to only increment when both endpoints pass that bar. Dangling refs
now disappear from issue_metrics the same way they already disappear
from issue_overview_mv.

New regression test (TestSQLiteExporter_DanglingDepIgnored) constructs
an export with a dangling DependsOnID and asserts
`blocked_by_count == 1` and `blocked_by_ids == "C"` — locks the
contract that the two surfaces stay aligned.

Verification: go test ./... clean; existing
TestSQLiteExporter_ResolvedBlockerExcludedFromCounts still passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
